title: OptiSparseMET: A Unified Framework for Sparse Multi-Environment Trial Design
text: "balanced_incomplete" method implements M4 allocation Montesinos-López et al. (2023). enforces two structural guarantees: Condition 1 — Equal replication: every non-common line appears exactly rr environments. ensures line systematically -evaluated defining property distinguishes M4 M3. Condition 2 — Equal environment sizes: every environment receives exactly k*k^* sparse lines. ensures environments comparable resource identity J*×r=×k*J^* \times r = \times k^* holds exactly. two conditions matter plant breeding programs thousands candidate lines tested across environments. Enforcing means every line gets number chances evaluated — fundamental fairness precision requirement. novice readers: Equal replication means two breeders select “top 10% lines” based trial data, working quality information every line. Without equal replication, lines tested often lower-variance estimates systematic advantage selection. allow_approximate = FALSE (default) enforces conditions strictly. slot identity J*×r=×k*J^* \times r = \times k^* must hold exactly, function stops clear error . Use check_balanced_incomplete_feasibility() verify slot identity allocating: Construction proceeds via crossdes::find.BIB() crossdes package installed, falling back greedy load-balanced constructor otherwise. allow_approximate = TRUE relaxes slot identity accepts minor replication imbalances. fallback exploratory use exact slot identity achieved, primary path.
code: # With k=39, J*=112, I=4, r=1: available slots = 4*(39-8) = 124, # required slots = 112*1 = 112. 124 ≠ 112 -> slot identity fails. check_balanced_incomplete_feasibility(   n_treatments_total             = 120,   n_environments                 = 4,   n_test_entries_per_environment = 39,   target_replications            = 1,   n_common_treatments            = 8 ) # feasible   = FALSE  (available = 124, required = 112, difference = 12) # Adjust k so that J*×r = I×k*: e.g. k=36 -> k*=28, 4*28=112=112*1.  # With k=36: slot identity holds. check_balanced_incomplete_feasibility(   n_treatments_total             = 120,   n_environments                 = 4,   n_test_entries_per_environment = 36,   target_replications            = 1,   n_common_treatments            = 8 ) # feasible = TRUE (difference = 0)

code: library(OptiSparseMET)  data("OptiSparseMET_example_data") x <- OptiSparseMET_example_data  # Stage 0: verify capacity k_safe <- suggest_safe_k(x$treatments, x$environments,                          common_treatments = x$common_treatments,                          buffer = 3)   # 39  # Stage 1: allocation # k_safe = 39 does not satisfy the slot identity for M4 (112*1=112 ≠ 4*31=124). # Use random_balanced (M3) here, or adjust k for M4 slot identity. # For M4 with r=1: k* = 112/4 = 28, k = 36. alloc <- allocate_sparse_met(   treatments                     = x$treatments,   environments                   = x$environments,   allocation_method              = "random_balanced",   n_test_entries_per_environment = k_safe,   target_replications            = 1,   common_treatments              = x$common_treatments,   allocation_group_source        = "Family",   treatment_info                 = x$treatment_info,   seed                           = 123 )  alloc$summary alloc$overlap_matrix   # environments x environments: how many lines shared  # Stage 2: seed-aware replication rep_plan <- assign_replication_by_seed(   treatments             = x$treatments,   seed_available         = x$seed_info,   seed_required_per_plot = 8,   replication_mode       = "p_rep",   desired_replications   = 2,   max_prep               = 15,   shortage_action        = "downgrade",   seed                   = 123 )  # Stage 3: design per environment (example for Env_A) design_A <- met_prep_famoptg(   check_treatments        = x$env_design_specs$Env_A$check_treatments,   check_families          = x$env_design_specs$Env_A$check_families,   p_rep_treatments        = rep_plan$p_rep_treatments,   p_rep_reps              = rep_plan$p_rep_reps,   p_rep_families          = x$treatment_info$Family[                               match(rep_plan$p_rep_treatments,                                     x$treatment_info$Treatment)],   unreplicated_treatments = rep_plan$unreplicated_treatments,   unreplicated_families   = x$treatment_info$Family[                               match(rep_plan$unreplicated_treatments,                                     x$treatment_info$Treatment)],   n_blocks   = x$env_design_specs$Env_A$n_blocks,   n_rows     = x$env_design_specs$Env_A$n_rows,   n_cols     = x$env_design_specs$Env_A$n_cols,   seed       = 123 )  # Stage 4: combine field books met_book <- combine_met_fieldbooks(   field_books       = list(Env_A = design_A$field_book),   local_designs     = c(Env_A = "met_prep_famoptg"),   replication_modes = c(Env_A = "p_rep"),   sparse_method     = "balanced_incomplete",   common_treatments = x$common_treatments )  head(met_book)

what: 14. Connectivity and Genomic Prediction: Simulation Illustration
title: OptiSparseMET: A Unified Framework for Sparse Multi-Environment Trial Design
text: benefit better cross-environment connectivity can explored simulation. following code generates sparse allocations M3 M4, simulates phenotypic data genomic model, fits GBLUP model dataset, compares resulting prediction quality. many settings uniform co-occurrence structure M4 leads lower average PEV stronger cross-environment connectivity. magnitude advantage depends degree imbalance, genetic covariance structure, ratio genetic residual variance.
code: library(sommer) library(MASS)  set.seed(1)  n_geno <- 200 n_env  <- 4  # Simulate a genomic relationship matrix G0 <- matrix(rnorm(n_geno^2), n_geno) G0 <- tcrossprod(G0) / n_geno diag(G0) <- diag(G0) + 0.5  simulate_MET <- function(allocation_matrix) {   geno_ids <- rownames(allocation_matrix)   env_ids  <- colnames(allocation_matrix)    # Simulate genetic values from GRM-based covariance structure   g_eff <- MASS::mvrnorm(1, mu = rep(0, length(geno_ids)), Sigma = G0)   e_eff <- rnorm(length(env_ids), 0, 1)    dat <- expand.grid(Geno = geno_ids, Env = env_ids, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)   dat$present <- as.vector(allocation_matrix)   dat <- dat[dat$present == 1, ]    dat$y <- g_eff[match(dat$Geno, geno_ids)] +             e_eff[match(dat$Env,  env_ids)]  +             rnorm(nrow(dat), 0, 1)   dat }  # M3 allocation: n=200, I=4, k=80, r~=1.6 (total=320, target=2 but approx) alloc_M3 <- allocate_sparse_met(   treatments                     = paste0("L", seq_len(n_geno)),   environments                   = paste0("E", seq_len(n_env)),   allocation_method              = "random_balanced",   n_test_entries_per_environment = 80,   target_replications            = 2,   allow_approximate              = TRUE,   seed                           = 1 )  # M4 allocation: equal replication (allow_approximate = FALSE). # J=200, I=4, r=2: k = J*r/I = 200*2/4 = 100. Slot identity: 200*2=4*100=400. alloc_M4 <- allocate_sparse_met(   treatments                     = paste0("L", seq_len(n_geno)),   environments                   = paste0("E", seq_len(n_env)),   allocation_method              = "balanced_incomplete",   n_test_entries_per_environment = 100,   target_replications            = 2,   allow_approximate              = FALSE,   seed                           = 1 )  dat_M3 <- simulate_MET(alloc_M3$allocation_matrix) dat_M4 <- simulate_MET(alloc_M4$allocation_matrix)  # Fit GBLUP model to each dataset fit_M3 <- sommer::mmer(   y ~ Env,   random = ~ sommer::vsr(Geno, Gu = G0),   data   = dat_M3 )  fit_M4 <- sommer::mmer(   y ~ Env,   random = ~ sommer::vsr(Geno, Gu = G0),   data   = dat_M4 )  # Compare mean PEV: lower is better (more precise prediction) mean(fit_M3$PevU$`u:Geno`$y) mean(fit_M4$PevU$`u:Geno`$y)

title: OptiSparseMET: A Unified Framework for Sparse Multi-Environment Trial Design

OptiSparseMET provides following: allocation level: two complementary strategies (M3 flexible approximate balance, M4 design-based uniformity), diagnostic tools verifying feasibility allocation attempted, genetic structure awareness family, GRM, pedigree-based grouping. field design level: two design engines complementary strengths (met_prep_famoptg() block-based repeated-check layouts, met_alpha_rc_stream() fixed-grid row-column designs), efficiency evaluation , D, CDmean criteria, criterion-driven optimisation via three methods (RS, SA, GA). Linking levels: seed feasibility verification ensures designs operationally deployable; common treatment support ensures design-based connectivity; pipeline functions (plan_sparse_met_design(), combine_met_fieldbooks()) ensure outputs levels consistent assembled unified MET field book. result workflow produces designs statistically grounded operationally deployable — rather optimizing one expense .

Equal environment sizes – every environment receives exactly k*k^* sparse treatments, resource identity J*×r=×k*J^* \times r = \times k^* holds exactly (J*=J−CJ^* = J - C, k*=k−Ck^* = k - C, CC number common treatments). guarantees distinguish M4 M3 paper. plant breeding programs thousands lines tested across environments, equal replication means every candidate evaluated number times – fundamental fairness precision requirement. allow_approximate = FALSE (default) enforces conditions strictly. slot identity J*×r=×k*J^* \times r = \times k^* hold chosen dimensions, function stops clear error allocation attempted. Use check_balanced_incomplete_feasibility() verify slot identity first, adjust kk rr identity holds. Construction proceeds via crossdes::find.BIB() crossdes package installed, falling back greedy load-balanced constructor otherwise. allow_approximate = TRUE relaxes slot identity allows minor replication imbalances. fallback exploratory use, primary mode.

code: library(OptiSparseMET)  treatments <- paste0("L", sprintf("%03d", 1:120)) envs       <- c("E1", "E2", "E3", "E4") common     <- treatments[1:10]    # 10 common treatments -> J* = 110  # Step 0: verify minimum capacity before allocating # J=120, C=10, I=4: J*=110, min k* = ceil(110/4) = 28, min k = 38 # suggest_safe_k adds a buffer of 3 on top of the minimum suggest_safe_k(treatments, envs,                common_treatments = common,                buffer = 3)           # returns 41  # Before M4 allocation, verify the slot identity holds. # With k=65, r=2, C=10: k*=55, J*=110. Identity: 110*2 = 4*55 = 220. Holds. check_balanced_incomplete_feasibility(   n_treatments_total             = 120,   n_environments                 = 4,   n_test_entries_per_environment = 65,   target_replications            = 2,   n_common_treatments            = 10 ) # feasible = TRUE: 110*2 = 4*55 = 220 (difference = 0)  # Step 1a: M3-inspired random balanced allocation alloc_m3 <- allocate_sparse_met(   treatments                     = treatments,   environments                   = envs,   allocation_method              = "random_balanced",   n_test_entries_per_environment = 41,   target_replications            = 1,   common_treatments              = common,   seed                           = 123 )  alloc_m3$summary$min_sparse_replication  # every treatment in >= 1 environment alloc_m3$summary$mean_sparse_replication  # Step 1b: M4 BIBD allocation -- slot identity must hold exactly. # J*=110, I=4, r=2: k* = 110*2/4 = 55, k = 55+10 = 65. # Slot identity: 110*2 = 4*55 = 220. Satisfied. alloc_m4 <- allocate_sparse_met(   treatments                     = treatments,   environments                   = envs,   allocation_method              = "balanced_incomplete",   n_test_entries_per_environment = 65,   target_replications            = 2,   common_treatments              = common,   allow_approximate              = FALSE,   seed                           = 123 )  alloc_m4$summary$min_sparse_replication  # 2: every sparse treatment in exactly 2 envs alloc_m4$summary$max_sparse_replication  # 2: equal replication confirmed  # Step 2: seed-aware replication plan seed_df <- data.frame(   Treatment     = treatments,   SeedAvailable = sample(10:100, length(treatments), replace = TRUE) )  rep_plan <- assign_replication_by_seed(   treatments             = treatments,   seed_available         = seed_df,   seed_required_per_plot = 10,   replication_mode       = "p_rep",   desired_replications   = 2,   max_prep               = 15,   shortage_action        = "downgrade" )  rep_plan$p_rep_treatments        # treatments receiving 2 plots rep_plan$unreplicated_treatments # treatments receiving 1 plot rep_plan$excluded_treatments     # treatments with insufficient seed  # Step 3: within-environment field design # For augmented / p-rep / RCBD-type block designs: design_E1 <- met_prep_famoptg(   check_treatments        = c("CHK1", "CHK2"),   check_families          = c("CHECK", "CHECK"),   p_rep_treatments        = rep_plan$p_rep_treatments,   p_rep_reps              = rep_plan$p_rep_reps,   p_rep_families          = rep("F1", length(rep_plan$p_rep_treatments)),   unreplicated_treatments = rep_plan$unreplicated_treatments,   unreplicated_families   = rep("F1", length(rep_plan$unreplicated_treatments)),   n_blocks = 4L, n_rows = 10L, n_cols = 12L,   seed     = 123 )  # For alpha row-column designs: design_E2 <- met_alpha_rc_stream(   check_treatments = c("CHK1", "CHK2"),   check_families   = c("CHECK", "CHECK"),   entry_treatments = rep_plan$p_rep_treatments,   entry_families   = rep("F1", length(rep_plan$p_rep_treatments)),   n_reps = 2L, n_rows = 8L, n_cols = 10L,   seed   = 123 )  # Step 4: combine into single MET field book met_book <- combine_met_fieldbooks(   field_books       = list(E1 = design_E1$field_book,                            E2 = design_E2$field_book),   local_designs     = c(E1 = "met_prep_famoptg",                         E2 = "met_alpha_rc_stream"),   replication_modes = c(E1 = "p_rep", E2 = "met_alpha_rc_stream"),   sparse_method     = "random_balanced",   common_treatments = common )  head(met_book[, 1:8])

what: Classify treatments into replication roles based on seed availability — assign_replication_by_seed
title: Classify treatments into replication roles based on seed availability — assign_replication_by_seed
text: Seed availability binding constraint determines whether target replication level deployable given treatment. function makes constraint explicit checking treatment per-plot seed requirement, optional minimum buffer, requested replication level, assigning roles accordingly. Treatments support target replication may downgraded unreplicated excluded design, depending shortage_action. Three design modes supported. "augmented" mode, treatments unreplicated; function identifies ones sufficient seed single plot flags rest exclusion. "p_rep" mode, subset treatments selected replication desired_replications remainder unreplicated; selection governed priority, restricted candidate_prep, optionally capped max_prep. "rcbd_type" mode, treatments targeted desired_replications; insufficient seed handled according shortage_action.

title: Classify treatments into replication roles based on seed availability — assign_replication_by_seed
text: assign_replication_by_seed() evaluates seed feasibility non-check treatment assigned single environment partitions treatments replication roles – replicated, unreplicated, excluded – according requested design mode available seed quantity per treatment. output intended direct input met_prep_famoptg(), constructs within-environment field layout resulting role assignments. Let \(s_i\) seed available treatment \(\), \(q\) seed required per plot, \(r\) requested replication count (desired_replications), \(b\) minimum seed buffer (minimum_seed_buffer). feasibility conditions : $$s_i \geq q + b \quad \text{(feasible one unreplicated plot)}$$ $$s_i \geq r \times q + b \quad \text{(feasible } r \text{ replicated plots)}$$ treatment fails first condition placed plot excluded regardless mode. treatment satisfies first second condition can support one plot target replication; fate shortage depends replication_mode shortage_action.

title: Classify treatments into replication roles based on seed availability — assign_replication_by_seed
text: "augmented": treatments targeted single unreplicated plot. Treatments satisfying \(s_i \geq q + b\) assigned role "unreplicated". fail assigned "excluded" shortage_action "exclude" "downgrade", trigger error shortage_action = "error". "p_rep": selection treatments designated replication desired_replications; remaining treatments unreplicated. Candidates replication drawn candidate_prep (treatments NULL) restricted satisfying replicated feasibility condition. Among candidates, selection priority determined priority: "seed_available" ranks descending seed quantity, "input_order" preserves order treatments, "random" draws uniformly. number replicated treatments optionally capped max_prep. Treatments selected replication evaluated single-plot feasibility assigned "unreplicated" "excluded". "rcbd_type": treatments targeted desired_replications. Treatments satisfying replicated feasibility condition receive role "p_rep" requested replication level. treatments fail, behavior depends shortage_action: "error" stops immediately; "exclude" drops treatment design; "downgrade" reduces treatment single unreplicated plot single-plot feasibility condition met, excludes otherwise.

those below threshold are downgraded to 1 rep or excluded. out2 <- assign_replication_by_seed(   treatments             = treatments,   seed_available         = seed_df,   seed_required_per_plot = 10,   replication_mode       = "rcbd_type",   desired_replications   = 3,   shortage_action        = "downgrade" )  out2$p_rep_treatments        # treatments with seed >= 30 #> [1] "L001" "L002" "L003" "L004" "L005" out2$unreplicated_treatments # treatments with 10 <= seed < 30 #> [1] "L006" "L007" "L008" "L009" "L010" out2$excluded_treatments     # treatments with seed < 10 #> character(0) out2$seed_summary #>    Treatment SeedAvailable Required_Unrep Required_Rep Can_Unrep Can_Rep #> 1       L001            50             10           30      TRUE    TRUE #> 2       L002            45             10           30      TRUE    TRUE #> 3       L003            40             10           30      TRUE    TRUE #> 4       L004            35             10           30      TRUE    TRUE #> 5       L005            30             10           30      TRUE    TRUE #> 6       L006            20             10           30      TRUE   FALSE #> 7       L007            18             10           30      TRUE   FALSE #> 8       L008            15             10           30      TRUE   FALSE #> 9       L009            12             10           30      TRUE   FALSE #> 10      L010            10             10           30      TRUE   FALSE #>            Role #> 1         p_rep #> 2         p_rep #> 3         p_rep #> 4         p_rep #> 5         p_rep #> 6  unreplicated #> 7  unreplicated #> 8  unreplicated #> 9  unreplicated #> 10 unreplicated  ## Example 3: augmented mode with a seed buffer -- each treatment gets one ## plot; a buffer of 5 seeds is reserved per treatment before feasibility ## is assessed. out3 <- assign_replication_by_seed(   treatments             = treatments,   seed_available         = seed_df,   seed_required_per_plot = 10,   replication_mode       = "augmented",   minimum_seed_buffer    = 5 )  out3$unreplicated_treatments # treatments with seed >= 15 #> [1] "L001" "L002" "L003" "L004" "L005" "L006" "L007" "L008" out3$excluded_treatments     # treatments with seed < 15 #> [1] "L009" "L010" out3$seed_summary #>    Treatment SeedAvailable Required_Unrep Required_Rep Can_Unrep Can_Rep #> 1       L001            50             15           25      TRUE    TRUE #> 2       L002            45             15           25      TRUE    TRUE #> 3       L003            40             15           25      TRUE    TRUE #> 4       L004            35             15           25      TRUE    TRUE #> 5       L005            30             15           25      TRUE    TRUE #> 6       L006            20             15           25      TRUE   FALSE #> 7       L007            18             15           25      TRUE   FALSE #> 8       L008            15             15           25      TRUE   FALSE #> 9       L009            12             15           25     FALSE   FALSE #> 10      L010            10             15           25     FALSE   FALSE #>            Role #> 1  unreplicated #> 2  unreplicated #> 3  unreplicated #> 4  unreplicated #> 5  unreplicated #> 6  unreplicated #> 7  unreplicated #> 8  unreplicated #> 9      excluded #> 10     excluded

text: balanced incomplete allocation exact, slot identity: $$J^* \times r = \sum_{e=1}^{} k_e^*$$ must hold, \(J^*\) number non-common treatments, \(r\) target number environments per treatment, \(\) number environments, \(k_e^*\) number sparse-allocatable slots environment \(e\) common treatments subtracted total capacity. environments equal capacity \(k^*\), reduces standard BIBD identity \(J^* \times r = \times k^*\). function evaluates condition returns logical indicator signed difference \(\sum k_e^* - J^* \times r\), quantifies degree imbalance exact feasibility fails. positive difference means available slots required – treatments receive extra replication approximate allocation. negative difference means slots insufficient – treatments -replicated. cases can handled allocate_sparse_met() allow_approximate = TRUE, magnitude difference informs whether resulting imbalance practically acceptable.

code: ## Example 1: exact feasibility -- 110 sparse treatments x 2 reps = 220, ## 4 environments x 55 sparse slots each = 220. check_balanced_incomplete_feasibility(   n_treatments_total             = 120,   n_environments                 = 4,   n_test_entries_per_environment = 65,   target_replications            = 2,   n_common_treatments            = 10 ) #> $feasible #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $n_sparse_treatments #> [1] 110 #>  #> $k_sparse #> [1] 55 55 55 55 #>  #> $total_sparse_slots #> [1] 220 #>  #> $required_sparse_slots #> [1] 220 #>  #> $difference #> [1] 0 #>  #> $message #> [1] "Exact balanced incomplete allocation is feasible: 110 sparse treatments x 2 replications = 220 sparse slots, matching the available total." #>  # feasible = TRUE, difference = 0  ## Example 2: slot deficit -- difference = -20. check_balanced_incomplete_feasibility(   n_treatments_total             = 120,   n_environments                 = 4,   n_test_entries_per_environment = 60,   target_replications            = 2,   n_common_treatments            = 10 ) #> $feasible #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $n_sparse_treatments #> [1] 110 #>  #> $k_sparse #> [1] 50 50 50 50 #>  #> $total_sparse_slots #> [1] 200 #>  #> $required_sparse_slots #> [1] 220 #>  #> $difference #> [1] -20 #>  #> $message #> [1] "Exact balanced incomplete allocation is not feasible: available sparse slots = 200, required sparse slots = 220, difference = -20." #>  # feasible = FALSE, difference = -20  ## Example 3: slot surplus -- difference = +20. check_balanced_incomplete_feasibility(   n_treatments_total             = 120,   n_environments                 = 4,   n_test_entries_per_environment = 70,   target_replications            = 2,   n_common_treatments            = 10 ) #> $feasible #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $n_sparse_treatments #> [1] 110 #>  #> $k_sparse #> [1] 60 60 60 60 #>  #> $total_sparse_slots #> [1] 240 #>  #> $required_sparse_slots #> [1] 220 #>  #> $difference #> [1] 20 #>  #> $message #> [1] "Exact balanced incomplete allocation is not feasible: available sparse slots = 240, required sparse slots = 220, difference = 20." #>  # feasible = FALSE, difference = 20  ## Example 4: heterogeneous environment capacities. check_balanced_incomplete_feasibility(   n_treatments_total             = 100,   n_environments                 = 4,   n_test_entries_per_environment = c(40, 45, 40, 45),   target_replications            = 3,   n_common_treatments            = 5 ) #> $feasible #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $n_sparse_treatments #> [1] 95 #>  #> $k_sparse #> [1] 35 40 35 40 #>  #> $total_sparse_slots #> [1] 150 #>  #> $required_sparse_slots #> [1] 285 #>  #> $difference #> [1] -135 #>  #> $message #> [1] "Exact balanced incomplete allocation is not feasible: available sparse slots = 150, required sparse slots = 285, difference = -135." #>

text: met_alpha_rc_stream() OptiSparseMET version alpha_rc_stream() OptiDesign package. met_ prefix avoids namespace conflicts packages loaded simultaneously. arguments, return values, internal logic identical alpha_rc_stream() OptiDesign. met_alpha_rc_stream() builds randomised alpha-lattice incomplete block design breeding agronomic experiments fixed field grid n_rows x n_cols plots. field converted one-dimensional planting stream according order serpentine, partitioned n_reps contiguous replicate segments, replicate divided incomplete blocks. Every incomplete block contains check treatments plus subset entry treatments. Entry treatments appear exactly per replicate. Trailing unused field cells remain NA layout matrix field book. Design evaluation separated construction. function returns field book, layout matrix, design metadata . compute -, D-, CDmean optimality criteria, call met_evaluate_alpha_efficiency() returned field_book. search optimised design using Random Restart, Simulated Annealing, Genetic Algorithm, call met_optimize_alpha_rc().

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — met_alpha_rc_stream
text: Let: \(r\) = number replicates (n_reps) \(c\) = number checks (length(check_treatments)) \(v\) = number entries (length(entry_treatments)) \(b\) = number incomplete blocks per replicate replicate contains \(v\) entries exactly \(c\) checks repeated \(b\) incomplete blocks. Entries split across blocks evenly possible - block entry counts differ one. number used plots per replicate : $$\text{plots per rep} = v + b \times c$$ total used plots design : $$\text{total used} = r \times (v + b \times c)$$ fixed field contains cells total, unused trailing cells remain NA. function stops required plots exceed field capacity.

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — met_alpha_rc_stream
text: Block-size constraints expressed terms total block size (checks + entries) repeated-check design whole block operational unit. mapping : $$\text{min\_entry\_slots} = \text{min\_block\_size} - c$$ $$\text{max\_entry\_slots} = \text{max\_block\_size} - c$$ example, \(c = 3\) checks, min_block_size = 19 max_block_size = 20 means block holds 16 17 entries plus 3 check positions. bounds must \(\geq c\).

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — met_alpha_rc_stream
text: translating total-block-size limits entry-slot limits, number incomplete blocks per replicate \(b\) must satisfy feasible range \([b_\text{min},\, b_\text{max}]\): Lower bound max_block_size \(b \geq \lceil v / (\text{max\_block\_size} - c)\rceil\) Upper bound min_block_size \(b \leq \lfloor v / (\text{min\_block\_size} - c)\rfloor\) Upper bound field capacity \(b \leq \lfloor (\text{total\_plots}/r - v) / c \rfloor\) function stops informative error \(b_\text{min} > b_\text{max}\). least one n_blocks_per_rep, min_block_size, max_block_size must supplied. User-fixed mode (n_blocks_per_rep supplied): value validated feasible range block-size constraints. Automatic mode (n_blocks_per_rep = NULL): \(b\) set \(b_\text{max}\) - largest feasible number blocks, producing smallest feasible blocks, generally gives best local control.

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — met_alpha_rc_stream
text: field mapped one-dimensional stream iterating rows (order = "row") columns (order = "column"). serpentine = TRUE, alternate rows columns reverse direction, producing boustrophedon planting path. Replicate segments occupy contiguous portions stream incomplete blocks occupy contiguous sub-segments within replicate.

title: Construct a stream-based repeated-check alpha row-column design — met_alpha_rc_stream

Checks inserted positions determined check_placement: "systematic": positions computed round(seq(1, block_size, length.= n_checks + 2))[2:(n_checks + 1)], distributing checks evenly possible. "random": positions drawn uniformly without replacement. Within-block order optimised attempts random shuffles minimise count adjacent -group treatment pairs. search exits early adjacency score reaches zero.

Number field rows (must match original design). n_cols Positive integer. Number field columns (must match original design). check_treatments Character vector check treatment identifiers (must match used met_alpha_rc_stream()). treatment_effect Character. Whether entry treatments modelled "fixed" (BLUE-based D criteria) "random" (PEV-based criterion CDmean). Note: "fixed" produce CDmean; "random" produce D-criterion. prediction_type Character. Random-effect prediction model entries. "IID" assumes independent entries (\(G^{-1} = \sigma_g^{-2} \)). "GBLUP" "PBLUP" use supplied K matrix (\(G^{-1} = \sigma_g^{-2} K^{-1}\)). "IID" (independence), "AR1" (row AR1 ), "AR1xAR1" (separable row x column AR1). rho_row Numeric \((-1, 1)\). AR1 autocorrelation parameter along rows. Used residual_structure %% c("AR1", "AR1xAR1"). rho_col Numeric \((-1, 1)\). AR1 autocorrelation parameter along columns. Used residual_structure = "AR1xAR1". varcomp Named list variance components. Required components: sigma_e2 (residual), sigma_g2 (entry genetic), sigma_rep2 (replicate), sigma_ib2 (incomplete block), sigma_r2 (row), sigma_c2 (column). components must present even corresponding effect absent model. sigma_g2 used denominator CDmean treatment_effect = "random". what: Construct a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — met_prep_famoptg
title: Construct a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — met_prep_famoptg
text: met_prep_famoptg() OptiSparseMET version prep_famoptg() OptiDesign package. met_ prefix avoids namespace conflicts packages loaded simultaneously. arguments, return values, internal logic identical prep_famoptg() OptiDesign. met_prep_famoptg() builds repeated-check block design plant breeding agronomic experiments. Check treatments included every block, non-check treatments allocated according user-specified replication levels. Depending treatment structure supplied, function generates three design classes: Augmented repeated-check design: checks repeated every block, test entries unreplicated (p_rep_treatments = NULL). Partially replicated (p-rep) repeated-check design: mixture replicated unreplicated non-check entries. RCBD-type repeated-check design: non-check entries given replication across blocks. Design evaluation separated construction. function returns field book, layout matrix, seed . compute , D, CDmean optimality criteria call met_evaluate_famoptg_efficiency() returned field_book. search optimised design across many randomisations call met_optimize_famoptg().

Must overlap p_rep_treatments unreplicated_treatments must contain duplicates. check_families Character vector length check_treatments. Family labels checks, used adjacency scoring. Always stored Family column; Gcluster always NA check plots. p_rep_treatments Character vector replicated non-check treatment identifiers. entry appears exactly p_rep_reps[] distinct blocks. May NULL character(0) augmented designs. p_rep_reps Integer vector length p_rep_treatments. Number blocks p-rep treatment appears . Must satisfy 1 <= p_rep_reps[] <= n_blocks \(\). p_rep_families Character vector length p_rep_treatments. Family labels p-rep entries. unreplicated_treatments Character vector treatment identifiers appear exactly full design. May NULL character(0). unreplicated_families Character vector length unreplicated_treatments. Family labels unreplicated entries. n_blocks Positive integer. Number experimental blocks. n_rows Positive integer. Number field rows. n_cols Positive integer. Number field columns. order Character. Field traversal direction: "column" fills column column; "row" fills row row. serpentine Logical. TRUE, alternate columns (order = "column") alternate rows (order = "row") traverse reverse direction, producing boustrophedon planting path. seed Optional integer. NULL, seed drawn 1:.Machine$integer.max returned seed_used. Controls randomised steps: p-rep block assignment, unreplicated entry distribution, within-block shuffling, check placement, dispersion optimisation. attempts Positive integer. Number within-block shuffle attempts minimise adjacent -group pairs. Default 1000. warn_and_correct Logical. TRUE, silently adjust field dimensions n_rows * n_cols match required total plots. FALSE, stop error. fix_rows Logical. Used warn_and_correct = TRUE. TRUE, hold n_rows fixed expand n_cols; otherwise hold n_cols fixed expand n_rows. cluster_source Character. Grouping source adjacency scoring: "Family", "GRM", "". GRM Optional square numeric matrix rownames colnames equal line IDs. Required cluster_source = "GRM" dispersion_source = "GRM". Optional square numeric matrix. Required cluster_source = "" dispersion_source = "". id_map Optional data frame columns Treatment LineID. Required treatment labels match relationship matrix rownames. cluster_method Character. Clustering algorithm: "kmeans" "hclust" (Ward's D2 linkage). cluster_seed Integer. Seed k-means initialisation, run isolated RNG scope. cluster_attempts Positive integer. Number k-means random restarts (nstart). n_pcs_use Positive number Inf. Leading PCs retained matrix-based clustering. Actual number used min(n_pcs_use, n_positive_eigenvalues - 1). check_placement Character. Check position rule within blocks: "random" Check positions drawn uniformly. "systematic" Check positions spaced evenly block. "optimal" Keeps layout maximises mean nearest-neighbour distance check plots field across check_opt_attempts candidates. check_opt_attempts Positive integer. Number candidate layouts evaluated check_placement = "optimal". Default 200. use_dispersion Logical. TRUE, apply post-layout swap-based dispersion optimisation reduce pairwise relatedness among neighbouring non-check plots. dispersion_source Character. Matrix used dispersion scoring: "K", "", "GRM". corresponding argument must non-NULL. dispersion_radius Positive integer. Chebyshev distance radius defining neighbourhood dispersion scoring. Default 1. dispersion_iters Non-negative integer. Number swap proposals dispersion optimisation. Default 2000. dispersion_seed Integer. Seed dispersion step, run isolated RNG scope. K Optional square numeric matrix. Used dispersion scoring dispersion_source = "K". line_id_map Optional data frame columns Treatment LineID. Required treatment labels match rownames(K) dispersion matrix. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints one-line summary field dimensions, block count, treatment counts.

title: Construct a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — met_prep_famoptg
text: Let: \(c\) = number check treatments \(b\) = n_blocks \(v_p\) = number p-rep treatments replication counts \(r_1, r_2, \ldots, r_{v_p}\) \(v_u\) = number unreplicated treatments Total plots required: $$\text{total} = b \times c + \sum_{=1}^{v_p} r_i + v_u$$ Allocation rules enforced construction: Checks Appear every block. check contributes \(b\) plots total. P-rep treatments treatment \(\) appears exactly \(r_i\) blocks, always distinct blocks - never twice block. core p-rep constraint. Unreplicated treatments Appear exactly full design, distributed evenly possible across blocks.

title: Construct a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — met_prep_famoptg
text: Augmented repeated-check design - obtained p_rep_treatments empty non-check entries supplied via unreplicated_treatments. Checks repeated treatments. P-rep repeated-check design - obtained non-check entries p_rep_treatments p_rep_reps > 1 others unreplicated. general use case: mixture replicated candidate entries single-plot entries alongside repeated checks. RCBD-type repeated-check design - obtained non-check treatments supplied via p_rep_treatments common replication count greater 1. count equals n_blocks, every non-check treatment appears every block - closest repeated-check analogue classical RCBD.

what: Compute the minimum per-environment capacity for full treatment coverage — min_k_for_full_coverage
title: Compute the minimum per-environment capacity for full treatment coverage — min_k_for_full_coverage
text: Let \(J\) total number treatments, \(C\) number common treatments assigned every environment, \(\) number environments. number sparse (non-common) treatments : $$J^* = J - C$$ Common treatments consume \(C\) slots every environment sparse allocation begins. number sparse-allocatable slots per environment : $$k_e^* = k_e - C$$ every sparse treatment appear least one environment, total number sparse slots across environments must satisfy: $$\sum_{e=1}^{} k_e^* \geq J^*$$ equal per-environment capacity, minimum number sparse slots per environment : $$k^*_{\min} = \left\lceil J^* / \right\rceil$$ corresponding minimum total entries per environment : $$k_{\min} = k^*_{\min} + C$$ function returns quantities. value min_total_entries_per_environment passed n_test_entries_per_environment guarantee full coverage minimum capacity.

Minimum number sparse-allocatable slots required per environment: \(\lceil J^* / \rceil\). min_total_entries_per_environment Integer. Minimum total entries per environment including common treatments: \(\lceil J^* / \rceil + C\). Pass n_test_entries_per_environment guarantee full coverage.

code: data("OptiSparseMET_example_data", package = "OptiSparseMET") x <- OptiSparseMET_example_data  env_trt <- x$treatments[1:24] env_fam <- x$treatment_info$Family[match(env_trt, x$treatment_info$Treatment)]  ## Example 1: p-rep repeated-check design — first 8 entries replicated ## twice, remaining 16 unreplicated. Checks in every block. out_prep <- prep_famoptg(   check_treatments         = c("CHK1", "CHK2"),   check_families           = c("CHECK", "CHECK"),   p_rep_treatments         = env_trt[1:8],   p_rep_reps               = rep(2L, 8),   p_rep_families           = env_fam[1:8],   unreplicated_treatments  = env_trt[9:24],   unreplicated_families    = env_fam[9:24],   n_blocks                 = 4,   n_rows                   = 6,   n_cols                   = 8,   order                    = "row",   serpentine               = TRUE,   seed                     = 123,   cluster_source           = "Family",   eval_efficiency          = FALSE,   use_dispersion           = FALSE ) #> Warning: Field size (6 x 8 = 48) does not match required (40). Adjusting dimensions.  dim(out_prep$layout_matrix)   # 6 x 8 #> [1] 6 7 head(out_prep$field_book) #>        Treatment Family Gcluster Block Plot Row Column #> plot_1      L008    F06     <NA>     1    1   1      1 #> plot_2      L012    F14     <NA>     1    2   1      2 #> plot_3      CHK2  CHECK     <NA>     1    3   1      3 #> plot_4      L015    F10     <NA>     1    4   1      4 #> plot_5      L002    F15     <NA>     1    5   1      5 #> plot_6      L007    F02     <NA>     1    6   1      6 out_prep$seed_used #> [1] 123  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## Example 2: augmented repeated-check design — no replicated entries, ## all 24 test entries unreplicated. out_aug <- prep_famoptg(   check_treatments         = c("CHK1", "CHK2"),   check_families           = c("CHECK", "CHECK"),   p_rep_treatments         = character(0),   p_rep_reps               = integer(0),   p_rep_families           = character(0),   unreplicated_treatments  = env_trt,   unreplicated_families    = env_fam,   n_blocks                 = 4,   n_rows                   = 5,   n_cols                   = 6,   order                    = "row",   serpentine               = FALSE,   seed                     = 123,   cluster_source           = "Family",   eval_efficiency          = FALSE,   use_dispersion           = FALSE )  dim(out_aug$layout_matrix) head(out_aug$field_book)  ## Example 3: RCBD-type repeated-check design — all 24 entries replicated ## 4 times (once per block). Equivalent to a classical RCBD with repeated ## checks. out_rcbd <- prep_famoptg(   check_treatments         = c("CHK1", "CHK2"),   check_families           = c("CHECK", "CHECK"),   p_rep_treatments         = env_trt,   p_rep_reps               = rep(4L, length(env_trt)),   p_rep_families           = env_fam,   unreplicated_treatments  = character(0),   unreplicated_families    = character(0),   n_blocks                 = 4,   n_rows                   = 14,   n_cols                   = 8,   order                    = "row",   serpentine               = TRUE,   seed                     = 123,   cluster_source           = "Family",   eval_efficiency          = FALSE,   use_dispersion           = FALSE )  dim(out_rcbd$layout_matrix) head(out_rcbd$field_book) } # }

title: OptiSparseMET 0.1.0
text: Added allocate_sparse_met() constructing treatment--environment incidence matrices using sparse testing principles. Supports "random_balanced" (M3) flexible approximate balance coverage-first guarantees. Supports "balanced_incomplete" (M4) BIBD-inspired uniform replication structure enforced equal replication equal environment sizes across trial. Accepts "M3" "M4" convenient aliases. title: OptiSparseMET 0.1.0

text: Supports "augmented", "p_rep", "rcbd_type" replication modes. Supports shortage_action values "error", "downgrade", "exclude" handling treatments insufficient seed. title: OptiSparseMET 0.1.0
text: Added met_prep_famoptg() constructing augmented, partially replicated (p-rep), RCBD-type repeated-check block designs. Key structural guarantees: Check treatments appear every block. Replicated (p-rep) treatments appear per block. Unreplicated treatments appear exactly across field. Optional genetic dispersion optimisation using GRM matrix. Optional within-environment efficiency evaluation (, D, CDmean). Added met_evaluate_famoptg_efficiency() evaluating statistical efficiency met_prep_famoptg() designs : Fixed random treatment effects. IID, AR1, AR1×AR1 residual covariance structures. -optimality, D-efficiency, CDmean, mean PEV criteria. Requires sigma_b2 (block variance) varcomp. Added met_optimize_famoptg() criterion-driven optimisation met_prep_famoptg() designs via Random Restart.

title: OptiSparseMET 0.1.0
text: Added met_alpha_rc_stream() generating alpha row-column stream designs suitable large structured fields. Key features: Repeated checks every incomplete block. Configurable block sizes via min_block_size / max_block_size fixed n_blocks_per_rep. Row-major, column-major, serpentine traversal orders. Optional genetic grouping family, GRM, matrix. Optional within-environment efficiency evaluation. Added met_evaluate_alpha_efficiency() evaluating statistical efficiency met_alpha_rc_stream() designs criteria met_evaluate_famoptg_efficiency(). Requires sigma_rep2 (replicate variance) sigma_ib2 (incomplete block within replicate variance) varcomp, distinguishing block-based evaluator. Added met_optimize_alpha_rc() criterion-driven optimisation met_alpha_rc_stream() designs via Random Restart, Simulated Annealing, Genetic Algorithm.

title: OptiSparseMET 0.1.0

Per-environment seed feasibility via assign_replication_by_seed(). Environment-specific field design via met_prep_famoptg() met_alpha_rc_stream(), selected design env_design_specs. title: OptiSparseMET 0.1.0
text: Implements sparse testing identity: making explicit tradeoff number treatments (J), number environments (), replication depth (r), treatments per environment (k).
